items.title: ReSum (RLVR + self-summarization)
items.innovation: Compresses reasoning via self-summarization to avoid overlong rollouts
items.analysis: Innovation: an RLVR framework where the model learns to self-summarize its reasoning trajectory mid-rollout, compressing long chains so they don't blow the context budget while keeping coherence. Performance: targets the 'overlong rollout degrades reasoning + wastes context' failure mode of vanilla RLVR. Fit for RL-on-NPU: shorter/compressed rollouts directly cut KV-cache memory during generation — exactly the pressure that bites on 64GB 910B (see the RL 显存争用 view).
items.summary: An RLVR framework that lets an LLM compress its reasoning via mid-trajectory self-summarization, avoiding overlong rollouts that waste context and hurt coherence (submitted Jun 11 2026).

items.title: FP8-RL: stable low-precision RL stack
items.innovation: Practical, stable FP8 stack for LLM reinforcement learning
items.analysis: Innovation: a practical FP8 rollout (and partial training) stack for RL, tackling FP8's instability in the RL loop with engineering + algorithmic fixes. Performance: reports stable low-precision RL with memory/throughput wins (paper-reported). Fit for RL-on-NPU: this is the missing recipe for the 'FP8 RL on Ascend' opportunity the 950 series opens — and the precision pitfalls it documents are exactly what align-probe should watch for on NPU.
items.summary: A practical, stable FP8 stack for LLM RL — addresses the instability of applying FP8 in the rollout/training loop via engineering and algorithmic techniques. Directly relevant to FP8 RL on next-gen NPUs.

items.title: ROLL Flash (async RLVR + agentic)
items.innovation: Asynchronous RLVR/agentic training with fine-grained parallelism
items.summary: Extends ROLL with a fully asynchronous training workflow and fine-grained parallelism to accelerate RLVR and agentic post-training — another point in the async/disaggregated RL design space.

items.title: TR-GRPO (Token-Regulated GRPO)
items.innovation: Token-level regulation stabilizes GRPO training
items.summary: Regulates per-token contributions in the GRPO objective to stabilize RL for LLMs, reducing variance/instability in long-sequence updates.

items.title: From Player to Master (test-time RL over memory)
items.innovation: RL over memory boosts agents' test-time learning
items.summary: Enhances LLM agents' test-time learning by doing RL over an external memory — letting an agent improve within a task/episode rather than only at train time.

items.title: Rethinking Agentic RL in LLMs (survey)
items.innovation: Frames agentic RL as a distinct paradigm vs single-turn RLVR
items.summary: A 2026 survey arguing agentic RL turns LLMs from passive responders into proactive agents — covering multi-turn credit assignment, tool/environment interaction, long-horizon planning, and reward design. A useful map of the agentic-RL landscape.

items.title: ProRL Agent (Rollout-as-a-Service)
items.innovation: Serves the full multi-turn agent rollout lifecycle as an API
items.summary: Scalable infrastructure for RL training of multi-turn LLM agents: exposes agentic rollouts (tool calls, environment steps) as a service so the trainer is decoupled from rollout orchestration — addressing the rollout bottleneck in agentic RL.

items.title: Stable Asynchrony (Variance-Controlled Off-Policy RL)
items.innovation: Tames heavy-tailed importance weights from stale rollouts
items.summary: Async RL decouples rollout from updates for throughput, but stale samples give GRPO/REINFORCE heavy-tailed importance weights and high variance. This work adds variance control to keep off-policy/async RL stable at high asynchrony.

items.title: RollMux (Disaggregated RL Post-Training)
items.innovation: Phase-level multiplexing to remove pipeline bubbles
items.summary: Builds on AReaL/StreamRL/AsyncFlow: multiplexes rollout and training phases across disaggregated resources to remove dependency bubbles while bounding sample staleness — directly relevant to NPU RL throughput.

items.title: GDRO-RL (Group Distributionally Robust Optimization)
items.innovation: Makes GRPO robust to prompt/rollout distribution shift
items.summary: Introduces Rollout-GDRO and Prompt-GDRO, which reweight groups toward the worst-case prompt/rollout distribution on top of GRPO, improving reasoning robustness when training data is heterogeneous or imbalanced.

items.title: G2RL (Gradient-Guided RL)
items.innovation: Lets the model guide its own exploration via gradient signals
items.summary: Uses gradient-guided exploration to steer GRPO-style sampling toward informative trajectories, improving sample efficiency on reasoning RL without a separate critic.

items.title: Critique-GRPO
items.innovation: Adds natural-language critiques on top of numerical reward
items.summary: An online RL framework that combines natural-language feedback with numerical rewards for policy optimization, reporting ~+15–22% Pass@1 gains over GRPO on Qwen models (revised Feb 2026).

items.title: InstructGPT / PPO-RLHF
items.innovation: Canonical 3-stage RLHF: SFT → reward model → PPO
items.summary: Established the standard RLHF recipe where a reward model trained on human preferences supplies the PPO signal, with a KL penalty to the SFT reference. A 1.3B InstructGPT was preferred over 175B GPT-3, making PPO the default RLHF optimizer.

items.title: GRPO (DeepSeekMath)
items.innovation: Drops the critic; advantages from group-normalized rewards
items.analysis: Innovation: removes PPO's value network — for each prompt it samples a group of responses and standardizes their rewards within the group to get advantages, eliminating the critic's memory and tuning burden. Performance: became the default backbone for reasoning RL (DeepSeek-V3/R1 and most 2025 RLVR work) precisely because it's stable and cheap at scale. Fit for RL-on-NPU: critic-free means less memory and fewer moving parts — ideal for memory-constrained 910B, and it's the algorithm MindSpeed-RL and verl-on-Ascend target first, so it's the natural baseline for any Ascend RL benchmark.
items.summary: For each prompt GRPO samples a group of responses and uses the within-group mean/std to compute relative advantages, eliminating PPO's value network and its memory cost. It is the backbone of DeepSeek-V3/R1 and the most widely adopted base for reasoning RL.

items.title: RLVR (Tülu 3)
items.innovation: Replaces the reward model with a deterministic verifier
items.summary: RL with Verifiable Rewards applies the RLHF objective to checkable tasks (math, instruction constraints), giving reward 1 for verified-correct outputs and 0 otherwise. Introduced/named in the Tülu 3 pipeline, it underpins most subsequent reasoning-RL work.

items.title: RLOO (REINFORCE Leave-One-Out)
items.innovation: Leave-one-out baseline; unbiased, low-variance, no critic
items.summary: Each completion's baseline is the average reward of the other samples for the same prompt, giving a simple multi-sample REINFORCE estimator. Trains ~3x faster with ~70% less memory than tuned PPO at 6.9B scale.

items.title: ReMax
items.innovation: Greedy-decoding baseline REINFORCE for RLHF
items.summary: Exploits RLHF's deterministic transitions and trajectory-level rewards with a greedy-decode baseline for variance reduction, dropping PPO's critic and 4+ hyperparameters. Cuts GPU memory ~46% on a 7B model while staying stable.

items.title: DPO (Direct Preference Optimization)
items.innovation: Preferences as a simple classification loss — no RM, no RL sampling
items.summary: Reparameterizes the RLHF reward in terms of the policy itself, reducing alignment to a logistic loss on preference pairs against a frozen reference. Matches or beats PPO-RLHF on sentiment, summarization, and dialogue while being far simpler.
items.analysis: Innovation: reparameterizes the RLHF reward in terms of the policy itself, collapsing alignment into one logistic loss on preference pairs against a frozen reference — no reward model, no sampling, no RL loop. Performance: matches or beats PPO-RLHF on summarization/dialogue/sentiment while being far simpler and more stable. Fit for RL-on-NPU: with no online rollouts it sidesteps the biggest NPU pain point (the missing vLLM sleep-mode) entirely — the easiest alignment method to stand up first on Ascend.

items.title: IPO (Identity Preference Optimization)
items.innovation: ΨPO theory; squared-loss objective robust to deterministic prefs
items.summary: Generalizes RLHF/DPO into a ΨPO objective; with Ψ=identity it avoids the Bradley-Terry assumption and DPO's overfitting on near-deterministic preferences. Provides a unified theory of preference learning.

items.title: KTO (Kahneman-Tversky Optimization)
items.innovation: Aligns from unpaired thumbs-up/down via prospect theory
items.summary: Maximizes a loss-averse human-utility function instead of preference log-likelihood, needing only a binary desirable/undesirable label per example. Practical for imbalanced or unpaired feedback while matching or exceeding DPO.

items.title: ORPO
items.innovation: Odds-ratio penalty folds alignment into SFT — no reference model
items.summary: Appends a log odds-ratio term to the NLL loss during SFT, removing the separate preference phase and reference model. Trained on UltraFeedback alone it surpasses larger Llama-2-Chat/Zephyr on AlpacaEval 2.0 and MT-Bench.

items.title: SimPO
items.innovation: Length-normalized average log-prob as a reference-free reward
items.summary: Aligns the implicit reward with the generation metric and adds a target reward margin, eliminating the reference model. Outperforms DPO and variants across Mistral, Llama 3, and Gemma 2 (NeurIPS 2024).

items.title: DAPO
items.innovation: GRPO + Clip-Higher, dynamic sampling, token-level loss, overlong shaping
items.summary: Targets entropy collapse and length/exploration issues in GRPO and ships as a fully open RL system. On Qwen2.5-32B it reaches 50 on AIME 2024, beating DeepSeek-R1-Zero-Qwen-32B with ~50% fewer steps.
items.analysis: Innovation: a fully open GRPO recipe fixing practical instabilities — Clip-Higher (decoupled clip bounds), dynamic sampling (drop all-correct/all-wrong groups), token-level loss, and overlong-reward shaping. Performance: 50 on AIME-2024 with Qwen2.5-32B at ~half the steps of prior baselines, with data + code released. Fit for RL-on-NPU: its tricks target the noisy-gradient and length-bias problems that worsen under NPU numeric drift, so it's a strong, well-documented baseline to port.

items.title: Dr. GRPO
items.innovation: Removes GRPO length & std-norm biases that inflate response length
items.summary: Diagnoses an optimization bias in GRPO that rewards longer (not better) responses and fixes it for a more token-efficient optimizer. A minimalist R1-Zero recipe with the fix hits 43.3% on AIME 2024 with a 7B base.

items.title: VAPO
items.innovation: Value-based PPO for long-CoT with length-adaptive GAE
items.summary: Reasserts the critic paradigm for long reasoning, combining DAPO/VC-PPO tricks with Length-Adaptive and Decoupled GAE plus positive-example LM loss. Scores SOTA 60.4 on AIME 2024 on Qwen2.5-32B in ~5,000 steps.

items.title: GSPO (Group Sequence Policy Optimization)
items.innovation: Sequence-level importance ratios & clipping — stabilizes MoE RL
items.summary: Attributes GRPO's instability to mis-applied token-level importance weights whose variance grows with length. Sequence-level optimization is more stable, notably for MoE RL, and contributed to Qwen3.

items.title: CISPO (MiniMax-M1)
items.innovation: Clips IS-weights, not token updates — keeps pivotal low-prob tokens
items.summary: Bounds variance via importance-sampling-weight clipping while keeping every token's gradient contribution, so 'reflection' tokens (Wait, Aha) still update. ~2x speedup over DAPO; enabled M1's full RL run on 512 H800s in ~3 weeks.

items.title: ProRL
items.innovation: Prolonged RL uncovers genuinely new reasoning strategies
items.summary: Stabilizes very long RL runs via KL control, reference-policy resetting, and a diverse task suite, showing prolonged RL expands reasoning boundaries rather than just amplifying base behaviors. Produced Nemotron-Research-Reasoning-Qwen-1.5B.

items.title: DeepSeek-R1 / R1-Zero
items.innovation: Reasoning emerges from pure RL (GRPO) with no SFT (R1-Zero)
items.summary: R1-Zero is trained by RL only and self-evolves chain-of-thought, reflection, and verification; R1 adds cold-start + multi-stage RL to reach o1-level (AIME 2024 pass@1 79.8%). The main reason GRPO+verifiable-reward RL became the 2025 paradigm.
items.analysis: Innovation: showed that large-scale RL with verifiable rewards (GRPO) alone — no SFT cold-start in R1-Zero — can elicit emergent long chain-of-thought, self-verification, and reflection. Performance: R1 reached o1-level math/code reasoning and, crucially, shipped open-weight, catalyzing the entire 2025 RLVR wave. Fit for RL-on-NPU: R1-Zero recipes are the canonical thing people reproduce on Ascend (small scale and 671B-MoE via MindSpeed-RL), making it the reference workload for any RL-on-NPU benchmark.

items.title: OpenAI o1
items.innovation: Large-scale RL teaches a long internal chain-of-thought
items.summary: Trained with large-scale RL to think productively before answering — decomposing, self-correcting, exploring alternatives. Performance scales with both more RL training and more test-time thinking.

items.title: OpenAI o3 / o4-mini
items.innovation: Scaled RL reasoning + agentic tool use inside the chain-of-thought
items.summary: o3 is OpenAI's most capable reasoner (~88% ARC-AGI, 96.7% AIME 2024); o4-mini is cheaper/faster. First o-series models trained to fully use tools (web, Python, vision) agentically within reasoning.

items.title: QwQ-32B
items.innovation: 32B dense rivals 671B R1 via scaled RL with rule-based verifiers
items.summary: Applies scaled RL from a cold-start checkpoint, first on math/coding with outcome verifiers then a general stage. Matches DeepSeek-R1-class reasoning at 32B, released Apache 2.0.

items.title: Qwen3
items.innovation: Unified thinking/non-thinking modes with a tunable thinking budget
items.summary: Dense + MoE (0.6B–235B) integrate reasoning and fast modes in one model via a four-stage post-training pipeline (long-CoT SFT + RL + fusion + general RL). Notes strong-to-weak distillation beating RL at ~1/10 cost for small models.

items.title: Kimi k1.5
items.innovation: Simplified RL scaling context length; no MCTS/value/PRM
items.summary: Multimodal model trained with a streamlined online-mirror-descent RL recipe that treats context length as a key lever. Matches o1 (77.5 AIME, 96.2 MATH-500) and adds long2short transfer to short-CoT models.

items.title: MiniMax-M1
items.innovation: Open hybrid (lightning-attention + MoE) reasoner trained with CISPO
items.summary: 456B total / 45.9B active, native 1M-token context; lightning attention makes long-CoT ~25% of R1's FLOPs at 100K tokens. Full RL on 512 H800s in three weeks (~$535K).

items.title: Llama-Nemotron
items.innovation: Open reasoning family with a dynamic reasoning on/off toggle
items.summary: Nano/Super/Ultra built via NAS + distillation then SFT and large-scale RL. LN-Ultra beats DeepSeek-R1 while fitting on a single 8xH100 node; models, data, and code released.

items.title: Phi-4-reasoning(-plus)
items.innovation: Small-model reasoning from curated SFT + short outcome-based RL
items.summary: 14B model SFT'd on 'teachable' prompts with o3-mini traces; the -plus variant adds a brief outcome-based RL stage that lengthens reasoning traces. Rivals DeepSeek-R1-class performance while far smaller.

items.title: Magistral
items.innovation: Ground-up async RL stack; strong reasoning from RL on text alone
items.summary: Magistral Medium trained by RL only (GRPO-based) on Mistral Medium 3; Small adds cold-start distillation (Apache 2.0). A rare detailed industrial account of scalable async RLVR infrastructure.

items.title: Seed1.5-Thinking
items.innovation: RL with a verifiable/general/hybrid triple data engine + stabilizers
items.summary: Compact MoE (20B active / 200B total) reaching 86.7 AIME 2024 and 77.3 GPQA, using value pretraining, decoupled GAE, and online data adaptation. Generalizes well beyond reasoning.

items.title: GLM-4.5
items.innovation: Hybrid reasoning MoE; ARC (agentic/reasoning/coding) post-training
items.summary: 355B total / 32B active MoE with thinking and direct modes, trained on 23T tokens with expert-model iteration + RL; 91.0% AIME 2024, 64.2% SWE-bench Verified. RLCS extends to multimodal in GLM-V.

items.title: Grok 3 (Think)
items.innovation: Reasoning refined via large-scale RL on the Colossus cluster
items.summary: Uses large-scale RL to think for seconds to minutes, backtracking and exploring alternatives via 'Think' mode, integrated with DeepSearch. Reports 93.3% AIME 2025, 84.6% GPQA.

items.title: verl (HybridFlow)
items.innovation: Hybrid-controller dataflow; first-class Ascend NPU support
items.summary: Supports PPO/GRPO/GSPO/DAPO/RLOO/PRIME and VLM RL with FSDP/Megatron training + vLLM/SGLang rollout, scaling to 671B. The only major framework with confirmed NVIDIA + AMD ROCm + Ascend support.
items.analysis: Innovation: a hybrid-controller dataflow separating single-controller RL orchestration from multi-controller distributed compute, making PPO/GRPO/RLOO pipelines composable — and it ships first-class Ascend NPU support. Performance: among the most widely adopted open RL frameworks, with strong throughput via colocated train/rollout. Fit for RL-on-NPU: this is the primary stack RL-on-Ascend work targets; its vLLM-Ascend rollout path is the realistic way to run GRPO on 910B today.

items.title: OpenRLHF
items.innovation: First Ray + vLLM RLHF framework; async agentic RL
items.summary: Distributes Actor/Critic/Reward/Reference across GPUs on DeepSpeed ZeRO-3 + Ray + vLLM, supporting PPO/GRPO/REINFORCE++/DAPO at 70B+ scale. Ascend support is WIP.

items.title: TRL
items.innovation: Tight Transformers/PEFT integration; simple GRPO/DPO trainers
items.summary: GRPOTrainer/DPOTrainer/PPOTrainer/RewardTrainer scale via Accelerate (DDP/DeepSpeed/FSDP). The easiest on-ramp for RL fine-tuning in the HF ecosystem.

items.title: NeMo-RL / NeMo-Aligner
items.innovation: Megatron-Core + Ray post-training to thousands of GPUs / 100B+
items.summary: Supports GRPO/DAPO/PPO/DPO/RM and on-policy distillation with Megatron parallelism and Ray orchestration. Influenced verl, SkyRL, and ROLL.

items.title: AReaL
items.innovation: Fully asynchronous RL: decouples generation from training (~2.77x)
items.summary: Rollout workers generate continuously while trainers update asynchronously with staleness-enhanced PPO. AReaL-lite offers an algorithm-first async API; uses SGLang for rollout.
items.analysis: Innovation: a fully asynchronous RL system that decouples generation from training so rollout workers never block on the trainer, attacking the idle/bubble problem of synchronous colocated RL. Performance: large throughput gains over synchronous baselines, at the cost of off-policy staleness that needs correction. Fit for RL-on-NPU: async decoupling is especially valuable on Ascend, where the missing vLLM sleep-mode makes colocated rollout/train memory-contended; AReaL has a dedicated Ascend branch.

items.title: slime
items.innovation: Thin Megatron↔SGLang glue — the RL stack behind GLM-4.5/4.6
items.summary: Wires Megatron training directly to SGLang rollout with minimal abstraction plus a Data Buffer module, validated on SOTA GLM releases.

items.title: ROLL
items.innovation: Multi-role Ray architecture for RLHF/RLVR/agentic
items.summary: Flexible resource allocation across roles integrating Megatron-Core, vLLM and SGLang for preference, RLVR, and multi-turn agentic RL. Ascend support is an open RFC.

items.title: SkyRL
items.innovation: Modular full-stack RL for multi-turn, long-horizon agents
items.summary: skyrl-train / -gym / -agent / -tx with async dispatching and backend interop (verl, Tinker); skyrl-tx aims at cross-platform backends.

items.title: PRIME
items.innovation: Implicit process rewards — dense per-token rewards, no process labels
items.summary: Online RL with process rewards from an ORM-trained PRM and no separate value model; integrated as an algorithm in verl.

items.title: prime-rl (Prime Intellect)
items.innovation: Decentralized, fully async off-policy RL (INTELLECT-2/3)
items.summary: Globally distributed async RL using FSDP2 + vLLM with FP8, PD disaggregation and EP/CP parallelism, plus TOPLOC rollout verification and SHARDCAST weight broadcast. Designed for 1000+ GPUs.

items.title: open-instruct
items.innovation: Introduced RLVR; trained Tülu 3 / OLMo 2
items.summary: SFT, DPO, PPO and GRPO-based RLVR scalable with Ray + vLLM. The reference implementation of verifiable-reward RL.

items.title: Unsloth (RL)
items.innovation: GRPO on a single small GPU (~5GB) via custom Triton kernels
items.summary: Memory-efficient GRPO with LoRA/QLoRA, vLLM-backed generation, long-context RL and FP8, optimized for single-GPU/local use.

items.title: Axolotl (RL)
items.innovation: Config-driven GRPO on TRL with async generation + replay
items.summary: GRPO/GDPO plus DPO with vLLM serving, importance-sampling correction, replay buffers, FSDP and DeepSpeed ZeRO-3 multi-GPU scaling.

items.title: Let's Verify Step by Step (PRM800K)
items.innovation: Step-level process supervision beats outcome supervision
items.summary: Trains a process reward model on per-step human feedback, solving 78% of a MATH subset and outperforming outcome-only RMs. Released the 800K-label PRM800K dataset anchoring modern process supervision.

items.title: Qwen2.5-Math-PRM
items.innovation: MC estimation + LLM-as-judge consensus filtering for PRMs
items.summary: Shows naive MC step labeling is noisy and BoN eval is misled by correct-answer/flawed-process cases, then fixes it with consensus filtering. Releases strong open PRM-7B/72B baselines.

items.title: Scaling Test-Time Compute
items.innovation: Compute-optimal inference can beat a ~14x larger model
items.summary: Analyzes how to spend a fixed inference budget across verifier-guided search and sequential revision, finding adaptive strategies beat best-of-N. Provides the scaling rationale behind long-CoT and verifier-driven RL.

items.title: ExGRPO
items.innovation: Prioritized experience replay for RLVR (off-policy)
items.summary: Buckets past trajectories by correctness/entropy and reuses high-value ones with a mixed on/off-policy objective. +3.5/+7.6 point gains over on-policy RLVR and stabilizes runs that otherwise collapse.

items.title: Search-R1
items.innovation: RL teaches interleaved multi-turn search within reasoning
items.summary: The model autonomously issues search queries during reasoning, trained stably by masking retrieved tokens from the loss. +41% QA accuracy (Qwen2.5-7B) over strong RAG baselines.

items.title: ReTool
items.innovation: Outcome-driven RL for strategic code-interpreter use
items.summary: Cold-starts on code-augmented traces then RL refines tool-invocation strategy with emergent code self-correction. Reaches 67.0% AIME 2024 (Qwen2.5-32B) in only 400 steps.

items.title: RAGEN / StarPO
items.innovation: Trajectory-level multi-turn agent RL; 'Echo Trap' stabilization
items.summary: StarPO trains agents across stochastic environments; StarPO-S counters reward-variance cliffs and gradient spikes via trajectory filtering and critic baselining. A systematic recipe for self-evolving agents.

items.title: Self-Rewarding Language Models
items.innovation: Model generates its own rewards via LLM-as-judge during iterative DPO
items.summary: The policy judges its own outputs to build preference pairs, so instruction-following and self-judging improve together. Iterated self-rewarding can surpass models trained on human preference data.

items.title: Spurious Rewards
items.innovation: Random/wrong rewards still boost RLVR on Qwen — but model-specific
items.summary: Random, format, or wrong-label rewards lift Qwen2.5-Math MATH-500 by 21–26 points (vs 29.1 for true rewards) by amplifying latent code reasoning, yet fail on Llama/OLMo. A caution that RLVR 'gains' may elicit pre-existing behaviors.

items.title: 1-shot RLVR
items.innovation: A single training example nearly matches thousands via RLVR
items.summary: One example raises Qwen2.5-Math-1.5B MATH500 from 36.0% to 73.6% with cross-domain generalization. Strong evidence that RLVR largely elicits latent capability, driven mainly by the policy-gradient loss.

items.title: Entropy Mechanism of RL
items.innovation: Clip-Cov / KL-Cov prevent policy-entropy collapse
items.summary: Links performance saturation to entropy collapse via log-prob/advantage covariance and clips/penalizes high-covariance tokens to sustain exploration. A widely-cited entropy-collapse fix.

items.title: TTRL (Test-Time RL)
items.innovation: Majority-vote pseudo-reward enables RL on unlabeled test data
items.summary: Estimates rewards at inference from self-consistency voting, needing no ground truth. Boosts Qwen2.5-Math-7B pass@1 on AIME 2024 by ~159% using only unlabeled test data.

items.title: Reward Shaping vs Reward Hacking
items.innovation: Bounded reward shaping (PAR) resists proxy over-optimization
items.summary: Unifies reward-hacking defenses (robust RM training vs RL regularization) and proposes Preference-As-Reward shaping using the RM's latent signal. Part of the active reward-robustness line alongside ensembles and uncertainty RMs.
